idea.hook: Our Milky Way: Is it really as flat as a pancake?
facts: The Milky Way's galactic disk spans an incredible ~100,000 light-years across.
Yet, its average thickness is only about ~1,000 light-years.
This gives our galaxy an astonishing width-to-thickness ratio of 100:1.
idea.payoff: If the Milky Way were a standard dinner plate (30cm wide), its disk would be just 3 millimeters thick! An unbelievably thin cosmic pancake of billions of stars!
idea.title: Milky Way: FLATTER Than a Pancake?! 🥞🌌

scenes.text: Our Milky Way: Flat as a pancake?
scenes.visual: Artistic rendition of the Milky Way galaxy, seen slightly from an angle in space.
Animated top-down view of a spiral galaxy. A measurement line expands across its diameter.
Transition to a side-on view of the galaxy. A measurement line highlights its vertical thickness.

scenes.text: That's an incredible width-to-thickness ratio of 100:1!
scenes.visual: A graphic combining the top-down and side-on views, clearly showing '100' next to width and '1' next to thickness.

scenes.text: If it were a 30cm dinner plate, its disk would be just 3mm thick! An unbelievably thin cosmic pancake of stars!
scenes.visual: A dinner plate appears, then a glowing, super-thin disc (representing 3mm) appears above it, shimmering with tiny star-like particles.

youtube.title: Milky Way: FLATTER Than a Pancake?! 🥞🌌
